message:
feat: implement true zero-copy tensor.view() with Arc-backed shared storage

Replace the previous view() stub (which just delegated to reshape()) with a
genuine zero-copy implementation that shares the underlying storage buffer via
Arc reference counting.

Core storage changes (ft-core):
- TensorStorage variants now wrap Arc<Vec<f32>> / Arc<Vec<f64>> instead of
  bare Vec, enabling cheap clone-by-refcount for view operations while
  preserving copy-on-write semantics via Arc::make_mut for in-place mutations
- Added DenseTensor::view() that creates a new tensor with a different shape
  but the same storage_id and Arc-cloned storage buffer — true zero-copy
- Added DenseTensor::shares_storage_with() for verifying storage sharing
- Updated from_storage_f32/f64, cast, and all TensorStorage constructors to
  wrap values in Arc::new()
- In-place mutation methods (set_values, set_values_f32) now use
  Arc::make_mut() to get exclusive mutable access, automatically cloning the
  buffer only when shared — correct copy-on-write behavior

Autograd changes (ft-autograd):
- Added TensorNodeOp::View variant to the op enum, distinct from Reshape,
  to track that the operation was a zero-copy view (not a data copy)
- view() method now calls DenseTensor::view() directly instead of
  delegating to reshape(), preserving storage sharing through the tape
- View op is included in all backward gradient propagation match arms
  alongside Reshape/Squeeze/Unsqueeze — gradient flows back correctly
- Updated CastF32/CastF64 ops to use Arc::new() when constructing storage

Dispatch changes (ft-dispatch):
- All ~24 typed dispatch functions (unary, binary, reduction, reduction_dim,
  pow, clamp, norm, norm_dim, scan_dim, normalize_dim) updated to wrap
  computed result vectors in Arc::new() when constructing TensorStorage

Tests:
- ft-api: 3 new integration tests for tensor_view (zero_copy_reshape,
  backward_through_view, numel_mismatch_errors)
- ft-autograd: 8 new unit tests covering same-shape view, flatten,
  unflatten, storage sharing verification, backward gradient correctness,
  wrong numel error, chained view-of-view sharing, and f32 dtype view
- ft-core: Updated all TensorStorage test constructors to use Arc::new()

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
